title: Sacred Heart Church, Santacruz
tagline: Sacred Heart Church, Santacruz — spiritual town of Maharashtra

overview.short: Sacred Heart Church is located on S.
overview.description: Sacred Heart Church is located on S. V. Road in Santa Cruz (Mumbai), Mumbai. The church, founded in 1936, is a Grade III heritage structure. The church has 8,000 parishioners and is part of the Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Bombay.

topPlaces.name: Ghanteshwar Temple Mumbai
topPlaces.description: The Ghanteshwar Temple is a Hindu temple to the god Hanuman in Khar (West), Mumbai, India.

topPlaces.name: Santacruz, Mumbai
topPlaces.description: Santacruz or Santa Cruz (Pronunciation: [saːn̪t̪akɾuːz]) is a suburb of Mumbai. The Santacruz railway station on the Mumbai Suburban Railway, the domestic terminal (T1) of the Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, and one campus of the University of Mumbai, are all located in Santacruz (East).

topPlaces.name: Khar, Mumbai
topPlaces.description: Khar (Marathi pronunciation: [kʰaːɾ]) is an affluent suburb of Mumbai, north of Bandra and south of Santacruz, Mumbai. Originally one of the 'villages' that made up the larger Bandra, the present suburb is divided into Khar West and Khar East areas.

topPlaces.name: Santacruz metro station
topPlaces.description: Santacruz Metro (ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund Santacruz Metro) is an underground metro station on the north–south corridor of the Aqua Line 3 of Mumbai Metro in Mumbai, India. This metro station was inaugurated on 5 October 2024, by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, followed by the commencement of the metro service from 7 October 2024.

topPlaces.name: Pali Hill
topPlaces.description: Pali Hill is an affluent residential area spread across the adjoined suburbs of Bandra and Khar in Mumbai, India (sharing postal codes 400050 and 400052 respectively).

topPlaces.name: Balgandharva Rangmandir
topPlaces.description: Balgandharva Rangmandir is a theater and cultural venue in Mumbai, India. Named after the Marathi theater actor and singer Bal Gandharva (1886–1967), the venue was redeveloped from a municipal open-air theater into an enclosed auditorium.

topPlaces.name: Late Bhausaheb Hiray Smaranik Samiti Trust
topPlaces.description: Late Bhausaheb Hiray Smaranik Samiti Trust (LBHSST) (also known as Hiray College Or Bhausaheb Hiray College) is an Indian college located at Bandra, Mumbai. Its course include Master's degree in Computer Application, Architecture, Applied Arts and Interior Design.

image.alt: Late Bhausaheb Hiray Smaranik Samiti Trust

topPlaces.name: Bandra Colony metro station
topPlaces.description: Bandra Colony is an underground metro station on the North-South corridor of the Aqua Line 3 of Mumbai Metro in Mumbai, India. It was opened to public on 7 October 2024.

items.note: The Ghanteshwar Temple is a Hindu temple to the god Hanuman in Khar (West), Mumbai, India.

Santacruz or Santa Cruz (Pronunciation: [saːn̪t̪akɾuːz]) is a suburb of Mumbai. The Santacruz railway station on the Mumbai Suburban Railway, the domestic terminal (T1) of the Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, and one campus of the University of Mumbai, are all located in Santacruz (East).

Khar (Marathi pronunciation: [kʰaːɾ]) is an affluent suburb of Mumbai, north of Bandra and south of Santacruz, Mumbai. Originally one of the 'villages' that made up the larger Bandra, the present suburb is divided into Khar West and Khar East areas.

Santacruz Metro (ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund Santacruz Metro) is an underground metro station on the north–south corridor of the Aqua Line 3 of Mumbai Metro in Mumbai, India. This metro station was inaugurated on 5 October 2024, by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, followed by the commencement of the metro service from 7 October 2024.
Pali Hill is an affluent residential area spread across the adjoined suburbs of Bandra and Khar in Mumbai, India (sharing postal codes 400050 and 400052 respectively).

Balgandharva Rangmandir is a theater and cultural venue in Mumbai, India. Named after the Marathi theater actor and singer Bal Gandharva (1886–1967), the venue was redeveloped from a municipal open-air theater into an enclosed auditorium.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sacred Heart Church, Santacruz?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sacred Heart Church, Santacruz is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sacred Heart Church, Santacruz?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai (3 km) and the nearest railway station is Mumbai CSMT (15 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sacred Heart Church, Santacruz?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sacred Heart Church, Santacruz at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sacred Heart Church, Santacruz?
faq.a: Stays in Sacred Heart Church, Santacruz start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sacred Heart Church, Santacruz?
faq.a: The top attractions are Ghanteshwar Temple Mumbai, Santacruz, Mumbai, Khar, Mumbai, Santacruz metro station, Pali Hill.
